title: 2026 Grammy Predictions: Who Will Win Song Of The Year - ChartMetric Data Analysis
text:
2026 Grammy Song of the Year Predictions

Based on Chartmetric data analysis, the article identifies three frontrunners:

Top Contender: "APT." by ROSÉ and Bruno Mars
- Chartmetric Track Score: 99.3
- Spotify streams: 2.27 billion
- The article notes this track "has maintained the most consistent CM track score of the three, remaining above 99.2 throughout the past year"
- Its cross-genre appeal bridging K-pop with Western mainstream gives it a strong cultural advantage

Secondary Contenders:
- "Luther" by Kendrick Lamar featuring SZA (98.9 score, 1.36B streams)
- "Abracadabra" by Lady Gaga (98.4 score, 701M streams)

Other Notable Nominees: The field includes diverse entries like "Golden" by EJAE, Audrey Nuna, and REI AMI (99.7 score) and "DtMF" by Bad Bunny (99.1 score), though the analysis suggests these tracks are stronger contenders in alternative categories.

Key Insight: According to the analysis, "sustained momentum, cultural impact, and cross-platform performance often point to who the Academy is most likely to reward."
